message:
feat(frankenterm-gui): bound headless rasterization + extend gpu_regression with stress fixtures

Two paired changes for the headless GPU regression harness — a bounds
fix in the production rasterizer and a meaningful expansion of the
test driver to cover stress and resize-sequence fixtures.

Production fix (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/headless_render.rs):

- rasterize_fixture_input now breaks the per-row and per-visual-row
  loops as soon as `row.saturating_mul(cell_h) >= height` — i.e., the
  first row whose pixel origin would land outside the framebuffer. The
  inner loop applies the same guard to visual_row so wrapped/over-wide
  text doesn't drive `draw_cell` past the buffer edge.
- Functional impact: fixtures whose declared text exceeds the rendered
  framebuffer height (the new stress fixtures, see below) are now
  rendered up to the available pixels instead of panicking on out-of-
  bounds slice indexing inside draw_cell. Behavior on already-passing
  fixtures is unchanged because the guard only skips iterations that
  would land outside the buffer.

Test driver expansion (crates/frankenterm-gui/tests/gpu_regression.rs):

- Args gains `fixture_filters: Vec<String>` so the harness can be run
  against a single fixture (or a name-prefix filter) without
  rasterizing the full corpus — speeds up local debug + targeted
  perf re-baseline runs.
- New `GeneratedLinesSpec { count, template }` allows fixture inputs
  to declare a procedurally generated multi-line block (count copies
  of template) instead of inlining each line into the JSON. Used by
  the new stress fixtures (many panes / many rows / wide content)
  the bead ft-ombfl.14 schedules.
- New `ResizeFrameSpec { second, width, height, dpi }` plus a
  `resize_sequence` slot on InputSpec lets a single fixture exercise
  a sequence of resize events with timestamps and per-step viewport
  dimensions, so the harness can pin behavior across mid-render
  resizes (ft's golden pixel-buffer-after-resize contract).
- `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "headless-render"), allow(dead_code))]`
  on the new spec structs so non-headless builds don't warn on the
  unused-by-the-feature definitions.
- Below the cut, the test driver wires the new specs into the
  existing render-and-diff loop and surfaces fixture_filters at the
  CLI argument level. Net delta: ~150 lines of test infrastructure,
  no production-runtime code beyond the rasterize fix above.

Lays the groundwork for the stress / resize-sequence golden fixtures
ft-ombfl.14 expects to land next; with this commit the harness is
ready to consume them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
